question: Which mathematician introduced the notation ∀ and ∃ that became standard in first-order logic?
What is the name of the boundary surrounding a black hole beyond which no information can escape?
Which treaty, signed in 1494, divided newly discovered lands outside Europe between Portugal and Spain?
Who wrote the medieval philosophical and theological work 'Summa Theologica'?
Which particle in the Standard Model is responsible for giving mass to other elementary particles through its associated field?
What is the capital city of the Federated States of Micronesia?
Which ancient Greek scientist is credited with the earliest known calculation of Earth's circumference using geometry?
In linguistics, what is the reconstructed common ancestor of most European and many Asian languages called?
Which composer completed exactly nine symphonies and is often associated with the 'Curse of the Ninth'?
Which chemical element has the highest electronegativity on the Pauling scale?
What is the name of the theorem proving that every map can be colored using no more than four colors so that adjacent regions differ?
Which explorer became the first known European to reach India by sea from Europe?
Which moon of Jupiter is the most volcanically active body in the Solar System?
Who formulated the laws of electromagnetic induction and introduced the concept of field lines?
What is the only letter that does not appear in the periodic table's one- or two-letter element symbols?
Which empire's official language was Classical Nahuatl during the reign of Moctezuma II?
Which mathematician solved the Basel Problem by proving that the sum of the reciprocals of the squares equals π²/6?
What is the deepest known point in Earth's oceans called?
Which philosopher is famous for the statement 'Cogito, ergo sum' ('I think, therefore I am')?
Which astronomical catalog object is also known as the Crab Nebula?
